operating_state: Caterpillar reported 2025 sales and revenues of $67.589 billion, representing 4% growth driven by higher equipment sales volume partially offset by unfavorable price realization. Power & Energy delivered strong growth led by data center applications and oil and gas services, while Construction Industries declined and Resource Industries remained flat. Order backlog surged to $51.2 billion from $30.0 billion, with significant portions extending beyond 2026, indicating extended delivery timelines across segments.
cost_structure: Operating profit margin compressed to 16.5% from 20.2% as manufacturing costs increased substantially, largely from incremental tariff impacts of $1.8 billion with 2026 exposure projected at $2.6 billion. Cost of goods sold rose to $44.8 billion from $40.2 billion, outpacing revenue growth despite operational efficiency gains. Restructuring costs increased to $445 million from $195 million, primarily related to Rail division inventory write-downs and facility consolidations targeting annual savings by 2027.
material_events: The company executed $5.190 billion in share repurchases including accelerated agreements, with $14.937 billion remaining under authorization. Issued $2.0 billion in senior notes comprising ten-year bonds at 5.2% and thirty-year bonds at 5.5%. Board approved the $790 million acquisition of RPMGlobal Holdings Limited expected to close February 2026, expanding mining software capabilities. Employment increased to 118,000 from 112,900 with growth concentrated internationally.
macro_exposures: Incremental tariffs created $1.8 billion manufacturing cost headwinds in 2025 with impacts expected to reach $2.6 billion in 2026, representing the primary new macro risk. Foreign exchange sensitivity indicates potential adverse impact from dollar weakening. Global operations remain exposed to trade policy volatility, supply chain disruptions, and commodity price cycles affecting customer capital expenditure decisions across energy, transportation and mining industries.
forward_outlook: Management expects 2026 sales growth around the top end of the 5-7% CAGR target supported by strong backlog and healthy end markets, with favorable price realization of approximately 2% anticipated. Capital expenditures projected at $3.5 billion with continued restructuring costs expected. The company maintains strategic focus on three growth pillars while accelerating investments in electrification, autonomy, and digital solutions.
uncertainty_profile: Tariff mitigation actions are planned though without these measures the impact could be 20% higher than projected levels. The company faces ongoing cybersecurity threats from sophisticated attacks targeting systems and connected products. Tax examinations by the IRS continue with unrecognized tax benefits, while Brazilian operations face litigation regarding labor and tax law applications and environmental remediation activities continue at multiple locations.
